This .NET solution has grown organically and the build infrastructure needs attention. Review all project files and suggest how to improve the build organization. Focus on reducing duplication, improving maintainability, and applying MSBuild best practices.

input.initialPrompt: This .NET solution has grown organically and the build infrastructure needs attention. Review all project files and suggest how to improve the build organization. Focus on reducing duplication, improving maintainability, and applying MSBuild best practices.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to review all project files in this .NET solution and suggest improvements to the build organization. Let me start by exploring the repository structure to understand what we're working with.

data.content:
Here's my analysis of the build infrastructure across all 5 project files:

## Issues Found

### 1. Massive property duplication
All 5 projects repeat these identical properties:
- `TargetFramework`, `LangVersion`, `Nullable`, `ImplicitUsings`
- `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`
- `Company`, `Authors`

### 2. Package version drift (no central management)
| Package | Project A | Project B |
|---------|-----------|-----------|
| `Logging.Abstractions` | 8.0.0 (Api, Core) | **8.0.1** (Services) |
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | 17.9.0 (Api.Tests) | **17.10.0** (Core.Tests) |
| `xunit` | 2.7.1 (Api.Tests) | **2.8.1** (Core.Tests) |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | 2.5.6 (Api.Tests) | **2.8.1** (Core.Tests) |

### 3. Duplicated custom target
The `PrintBuildInfo` target is copy-pasted in all 3 src projects.

### 4. Duplicated `DocumentationFile` setting
Repeated in all 3 src projects.

### 5. No `Directory.Build.props` or `.targets` files exist

---

## Recommended Fix

**Introduce 3 files** to centralize everything:

### `Directory.Build.props` (repo root)
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Source projects generate XML docs -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Test projects are not packable -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

### `Directory.Build.targets` (repo root)
```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
          Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

### `Directory.Packages.props` (repo root — enables Central Package Management)
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <!-- Test -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

### Resulting slimmed-down project files

After this refactor, e.g. `ContosoApp.Core.csproj` becomes:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

And `ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj`:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

---

## Summary of Benefits

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| 7 duplicated properties × 5 files | Set once in `Directory.Build.props` |
| Version drift across test projects | Single source of truth in `Directory.Packages.props` |
| Custom target pasted 3× | Defined once in `Directory.Build.targets` |
| ~30 lines per project | ~5–12 lines per project |

Would you like me to implement these changes?
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me analyze the issues:

1. **Duplicated properties across all 5 projects**: `TargetFramework`, `LangVersion`, `Nullable`, `ImplicitUsings`, `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`, `Company`, `Authors` are copy-pasted in every project.

2. **No `Directory.Build.props`**: There's no centralized build configuration file. This is the primary MSBuild mechanism for sharing properties.

3. **No `Directory.Packages.props` (Central Package Management)**: Package versions are scattered across project files, and they're already drifting:
   - `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` ranges from `8.0.0` to `8.0.1` across projects
   - `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` varies between `17.9.0` and `17.10.0`
   - `xunit` differs from `2.7.1` to `2.8.1`
   - `xunit.runner.visualstudio` is inconsistent at `2.5.6` versus `2.8.1`

4. **Duplicated custom targets** are likely also scattered throughout the project files.

5. **Duplicated properties** like `DocumentationFile` are repeated across all source projects.

6. **Test infrastructure is duplicated** between test projects with version mismatches.

7. **Solution organization is missing** — no folders grouping the source and test projects together.

I should provide a comprehensive set of recommendations to address these issues.
